stories.title: Run base-35B benchmark on all domains
stories.description: Run `scripts/benchmark_base_vs_lora.py` against all 32 original domains to confirm which ones the base 35B-A3B already handles. Expected outcome: 22 domains classified as 'known' (loss delta < 5% vs specialist baseline), 10 domains showing genuine gap. Output: `results/benchmark_base_vs_lora.json`. Acceptance: file exists, at least 10 domains flagged as gap domains, results reproducible.

stories.title: Merge datasets for 10 niches
stories.description: Run `scripts/merge_datasets.py --all` on Mac Studio. For each of the 10 KEEP domains (kicad-dsl, spice, emc, stm32, embedded, freecad, platformio, power, dsp, electronics): download HF mascarade enrichment sets, deduplicate against KIKI-Mac_tunner splits using SHA-256 on normalized prompt text, re-split 80/10/10. Flag any domain with merged total < 2,000 examples for teacher augmentation. Output: `data/merged/<domain>/` with train/valid/test jsonl. Acceptance: 10 domain dirs present, each with split files.

stories.title: Validate benchmark results — confirm 22 known domains
stories.description: Analyse `results/benchmark_base_vs_lora.json` output from story-1 and validate: confirm exactly 22 domains can be served by passthrough (base model), confirm 10 domains have measurable gap requiring LoRA. Update `docs/specs/2026-04-16-reorientation-rationale.md` with final counts and champion/challenger metric table. Acceptance: doc updated, gap count matches 10 niche domains, any deviation documented with rationale.

stories.title: Train niche stack: kicad-dsl
stories.description: Train LoRA stack for domain `kicad-dsl` using merged dataset from story-2. Config: rank 16, alpha 32, q/k/v/o projections, 3-phase curriculum (seq 512→1280→4096), LR 8e-6→5e-6→3e-6, OPLoRA init. Run forgetting check after training; rollback if angle < 30° AND win-rate drop > 0.03. Output adapter to `outputs/stacks/stack-01-kicad-dsl/`. Acceptance: training completes, eval BLEU/exact-match reported, forgetting check pass.

stories.title: Train niche stack: spice
stories.description: Train LoRA stack for domain `spice` using merged dataset from story-2. Same config as story-4 (rank 16, OPLoRA). Run forgetting check. Output adapter to `outputs/stacks/stack-02-spice/`. Acceptance: training completes, eval reported, forgetting check pass.

stories.title: Train niche stack: emc
stories.description: Train LoRA stack for domain `emc` using merged dataset from story-2. Same config as story-4 (rank 16, OPLoRA). Run forgetting check. Output adapter to `outputs/stacks/stack-03-emc/`. Acceptance: training completes, eval reported, forgetting check pass.

stories.title: Train niche stack: stm32
stories.description: Train LoRA stack for domain `stm32` using merged dataset from story-2. Rank 16, OPLoRA. stm32 is marginal (~2,723 examples) — apply early stopping with patience 3. Run forgetting check. Output adapter to `outputs/stacks/stack-04-stm32/`. Acceptance: training completes, early stopping triggered or not noted, eval reported, forgetting check pass.

stories.title: Train niche stack: embedded
stories.description: Train LoRA stack for domain `embedded` using merged dataset from story-2 (~13,826 examples — strongest case). Rank 16, OPLoRA. Run forgetting check. Output adapter to `outputs/stacks/stack-05-embedded/`. Acceptance: training completes, eval reported, forgetting check pass.

stories.title: Train niche stack: freecad
stories.description: Train LoRA stack for domain `freecad` using merged + teacher-augmented dataset (target ≥ 1,500 examples). Use rank 8 (high params/example ratio). OPLoRA init. Run forgetting check. Output adapter to `outputs/stacks/stack-06-freecad/`. Acceptance: training completes, augmentation count documented, eval reported, forgetting check pass.

stories.title: Train niche stack: platformio
stories.description: Train LoRA stack for domain `platformio` using merged + teacher-augmented dataset (target ≥ 1,500 examples). Use rank 8. OPLoRA init. Run forgetting check. Output adapter to `outputs/stacks/stack-07-platformio/`. Acceptance: training completes, augmentation count documented, eval reported, forgetting check pass.

stories.title: Train niche stack: power
stories.description: Train LoRA stack for domain `power` using merged dataset from story-2. Rank 16, OPLoRA. Run forgetting check. Output adapter to `outputs/stacks/stack-08-power/`. Acceptance: training completes, eval reported, forgetting check pass.

stories.title: Train niche stack: dsp
stories.description: Train LoRA stack for domain `dsp` using merged dataset from story-2. Rank 16, OPLoRA. Run forgetting check. Output adapter to `outputs/stacks/stack-09-dsp/`. Acceptance: training completes, eval reported, forgetting check pass.

stories.title: Train niche stack: electronics
stories.description: Train LoRA stack for domain `electronics` using merged dataset from story-2 (~1,900 examples — high overfitting risk). Use rank 8 to halve trainable params. Early stopping patience 3. Run forgetting check. Output adapter to `outputs/stacks/stack-10-electronics/`. Acceptance: training completes, overfitting status noted, eval reported, forgetting check pass.

stories.title: Forgetting check across all 10 stacks
stories.description: After all 10 stacks are trained, run a comprehensive cross-stack forgetting analysis. For each stack pair, compute cosine angle between LoRA update subspaces. Flag any pair with angle < 30°. Generate report at `results/forgetting_cross_stacks.json`. Acceptance: report exists, all angle readings above 30° or rollback decisions documented.

stories.title: Eval all 10 stacks vs base
stories.description: Run the full eval suite comparing each of the 10 trained stacks against the base 35B-A3B passthrough on domain-specific held-out test sets. Report win-rate, BLEU, exact-match where applicable. Generate `results/stacks_vs_base_eval.json`. Acceptance: all 10 stacks show win-rate > 0.50 vs base on their target domain, or are flagged for retraining with revised config.

stories.title: Deploy 11-output domain router
stories.description: Deploy the 11-output domain classifier router (10 domain scores + 1 passthrough head). Router code is already implemented in `scripts/micro_kiki/meta_router.py`. Validate: load router, run inference on sample prompts from all 10 domains + 5 passthrough prompts, confirm correct routing. Acceptance: routing accuracy ≥ 80% on 50-prompt smoke test, passthrough triggered correctly on general queries.

stories.title: Deploy multi-model router
stories.description: Deploy the multi-model router that selects among 35B-A3B+LoRA (hardware/EDA), 35B-A3B passthrough (general), Qwen3-Coder-480B (complex synthesis), devstral-v3 (firmware). Router code already implemented. Validate: each routing tier reachable, health-check endpoints return 200. Acceptance: all 4 tiers respond, routing decision log shows expected tier selection on test prompts.

stories.title: Integration test router → adapter → response
stories.description: End-to-end integration test of the routing chain: domain router → adapter load → model inference → response. Test all 10 domain paths + passthrough path. Measure p50/p95 latency for adapter hot-swap. Acceptance: all 11 paths return valid non-empty responses, adapter swap latency p95 < 500ms, no OOM errors.

stories.title: E2E smoke test: 10 domains + base fallback
stories.description: Full E2E smoke test simulating a realistic usage session: send 2 prompts per domain (20 hardware/EDA prompts) + 5 general prompts that should hit passthrough. Validate response quality (non-empty, domain-coherent, no hallucinated footprints/registers on spot-check). Acceptance: all 25 prompts return responses, routing log confirms correct tier for each, spot-check 5 responses manually noted as coherent.

stories.title: Aeon memory integration test
stories.description: Integration test for the Aeon memory palace layer: inject a multi-turn hardware design session (5 turns referencing KiCad component names), then query recall. Verify Atlas SIMD index returns correct memories, Trace graph records temporal relationships. Acceptance: recall precision ≥ 0.8 on 10-query test set, no backend connection errors (native or Qdrant/Neo4j).

stories.title: Negotiator integration test
stories.description: Integration test for the Negotiator layer (CAMP arbitration + Catfish dissent). Create a scenario where two domain stacks produce conflicting recommendations (e.g., kicad-dsl vs power on a power delivery network question). Verify CAMP produces a reasoned arbitration, Catfish injects structured dissent. Acceptance: arbitration output is non-trivial, dissent correctly flags when stacks agree silently, judge selection (fast/deep) respects config.

stories.title: Anti-bias pipeline test
stories.description: Test KnowBias double-application and RBD runtime detector on 20 bias-probe prompts from the 5,213-pair dataset. Verify: RBD flags biased responses, DeFrame re-generation is triggered on flagged responses, re-generated responses score lower on bias probe. Acceptance: bias detection precision ≥ 0.75 on held-out probe set, DeFrame reduces bias score by ≥ 20% on flagged samples.

stories.title: Full cognitive layer E2E
stories.description: End-to-end test of the complete cognitive pipeline: Aeon recall → domain router → stacks → Negotiator → Anti-bias → Aeon write. Use a 10-turn hardware design session touching at least 3 domains. Verify all layers activate correctly, no layer is bypassed silently. Acceptance: all cognitive layer components appear in trace log, session concludes without errors, final response passes spot-check.

stories.title: Anti-bias dataset eval (5213 pairs)
stories.description: Full evaluation run of the anti-bias pipeline on the complete 5,213-pair bias dataset. Generate metrics: precision, recall, F1 for RBD detector; bias score delta for DeFrame re-generation; false positive rate. Output: `results/anti_bias_eval_full.json`. Acceptance: RBD F1 ≥ 0.70, DeFrame bias delta ≥ 15% average across flagged samples.

stories.title: LAS convert Qwen3.5-27B → SpikingKiki-27B
stories.description: Run Latency-Aware Spiking (LAS) conversion on Qwen3.5-27B to produce SpikingKiki-27B. Estimated ~30h on Mac Studio. Use LAS conversion scripts in `src/spiking/`. Output SNN weights to `outputs/snn/spikingkiki-27b/`. Monitor membrane potential convergence and firing rate statistics. Acceptance: conversion completes without NaN loss, firing rate histogram saved, model loadable for inference.

stories.title: Eval SpikingKiki-27B
stories.description: Evaluate SpikingKiki-27B on the 10-domain niche test suite and compare to Qwen3.5-27B dense baseline. Measure perplexity, task accuracy, and energy proxy (spike count × synaptic weight as theoretical energy estimate). Output: `results/snn_eval_27b.json`. Acceptance: perplexity delta < 10% vs dense baseline, energy proxy reduction documented.

stories.title: LAS convert Qwen3.5-35B-A3B → SpikingKiki-35B (MoE)
stories.description: Run LAS conversion on Qwen3.5-35B-A3B MoE to produce SpikingKiki-35B. Estimated ~40h on Mac Studio. MoE routing layers require special handling: apply spiking conversion only to attention projections, preserve MoE gate logic as dense. Output to `outputs/snn/spikingkiki-35b/`. Acceptance: conversion completes, MoE routing still functional post-conversion (gate outputs verified on test prompts).

stories.title: Eval SpikingKiki-35B
stories.description: Evaluate SpikingKiki-35B on the 10-domain niche test suite and compare to Qwen3.5-35B-A3B dense baseline. Include comparison with SpikingKiki-27B. Output: `results/snn_eval_35b.json`. Acceptance: perplexity delta < 10% vs dense baseline, MoE routing accuracy preserved ≥ 95% vs dense.

stories.title: LAS convert Qwen3.5-122B-A10B (if available)
stories.description: If Qwen3.5-122B-A10B weights are available on Mac Studio, run LAS conversion to produce SpikingKiki-122B. Estimated ~100h. This story is conditional: if weights are unavailable, mark as skipped with explanation in progress.txt. Output to `outputs/snn/spikingkiki-122b/`. Acceptance: conversion completes or skip documented with reason.

stories.title: Cross-eval all SNN variants
stories.description: Run a cross-evaluation of all available SNN variants (SpikingKiki-27B, SpikingKiki-35B, SpikingKiki-122B if available) on a unified benchmark: 10-domain niche tasks + general reasoning (MMLU subset). Generate comparison table. Output: `results/snn_cross_eval.json`. Acceptance: all available variants evaluated, comparison table includes quality vs energy tradeoff.

stories.title: Energy benchmark: theoretical + measured
stories.description: Produce energy efficiency report for all SNN variants vs dense baselines. Theoretical: spike count × synaptic weight proxy. Measured: wall-clock inference time + power draw (via powermetrics on Mac Studio or NVML on kxkm-ai). Output: `results/energy_benchmark.json` + `docs/research/snn-energy-report.md`. Acceptance: both theoretical and measured metrics present for at least 2 SNN variants, comparison vs dense documented.

stories.title: Spikingformer cross-validation
stories.description: Cross-validate SpikingKiki models against Spikingformer architecture reference results on common benchmarks. Identify divergences and document root causes. Output: `results/spikingformer_crossval.json`. Acceptance: cross-validation complete, ≥ 3 benchmark tasks compared, divergences ≥ 10% annotated with cause.

stories.title: MLX server with niche adapters
stories.description: MLX serving pipeline with dynamic LoRA adapter loading for 10 niche stacks. Server code already implemented in `src/serving/`. Validate: start MLX server, load each of the 10 adapters on demand, verify hot-swap works, no memory leak across swaps. Acceptance: all 10 adapters load/unload without OOM, inference latency within spec.

stories.title: vLLM server with niche adapters
stories.description: vLLM Q4 inference server on kxkm-ai with dynamic LoRA for 10 niche stacks. Server code already implemented in `src/serving/`. Validate: start vLLM server, load Q4_K_M quantized base + adapters, verify adapter routing, check GPU memory usage stays within 24 GB. Acceptance: all 10 adapters loadable, GPU memory < 22 GB with 2 adapters active simultaneously.

stories.title: Service units: launchd + systemd
stories.description: Service unit files for automated startup: launchd plists for Mac Studio (MLX server + router), systemd units for Tower (Aeon backends) and kxkm-ai (vLLM). Deploy files already exist in `deploy/`. Validate: launchd load on Mac Studio, systemd enable on relevant machines. Acceptance: services start on boot (tested via reboot simulation with `launchctl start` / `systemctl start`), health endpoints return 200 within 60s of start.

stories.title: Integration test serving pipeline
stories.description: Full integration test of the serving pipeline end-to-end: MLX server + vLLM server + router + cognitive layer all running simultaneously. Send 20 test prompts covering all 10 domains + passthrough. Measure total latency (p50/p95), verify correct adapter selection, verify cognitive layer active. Acceptance: all prompts return responses, p95 latency < 10s for hardware domain queries, no serving errors in logs.

stories.title: Freeze config + migration guide
stories.description: Freeze all production configs (router, serving, cognitive layer) and write migration guide from v0.2 (32 stacks) to v0.3 (10 niche stacks). Config freeze already done. Migration guide covers: dropped stacks → passthrough, router update steps, dataset cleanup (22 dropped domain dirs), adapter artifact removal. Acceptance: config tagged in git, migration guide exists at `docs/migration/v0.2-to-v0.3.md`.

stories.title: Model card + cookbook
stories.description: Write HuggingFace model card for micro-kiki v0.3 (10 niche LoRA adapters + SNN variants) and a usage cookbook with examples for each of the 10 domains. Model card and cookbook templates already drafted. Acceptance: `docs/release/model_card.md` and `docs/release/cookbook.md` exist with domain-specific examples for all 10 niches.

stories.title: HuggingFace release: niche adapters
stories.description: Publish the 10 trained LoRA adapters to HuggingFace Hub under `electron-rare/micro-kiki-adapters`. Include model card, eval metrics, and per-domain README. Acceptance: all 10 adapters visible on HF Hub, model card rendered correctly, download test passes (`huggingface_hub.snapshot_download` retrieves files).

stories.title: HuggingFace release: SNN models
stories.description: Publish available SpikingKiki SNN models to HuggingFace Hub under `electron-rare/spikingkiki`. Include energy benchmark results, architecture notes, and comparison vs dense baseline. Acceptance: at least SpikingKiki-27B and SpikingKiki-35B published (122B if available), energy report attached to model card, download test passes.

stories.title: DPO preference pairs via 480B judge
stories.description: Generate preference pairs (chosen/rejected) for each niche domain using the 480B teacher as judge. For each SFT-trained domain, generate 500 prompts → 2 responses (480B + SFT model), 480B judges which is better. Output: data/dpo/<domain>/train.jsonl with {prompt, chosen, rejected}. Script: scripts/generate_dpo_pairs.py. Uses llama.cpp CPU 480B on port 8481.

stories.title: DPO training script (mlx-tune DPOTrainer)
stories.description: Create scripts/train_dpo_niches.py using mlx-tune DPOTrainer. For each niche domain, load SFT adapter → apply DPO on preference pairs. Adaptive LR (1e-6 for DPO, lower than SFT). Metal buffer fixes (set_cache_limit). Output: outputs/stacks/stack-<domain>-dpo/adapters.safetensors.

stories.title: DPO training 10 niches on Studio
stories.description: Run scripts/train_dpo_niches.py --all on Studio M3 Ultra. Sequential, ~30 min per domain. Eval: compare DPO vs SFT-only on 50 eval prompts per domain. Expected: +5-10% quality on structured outputs (SPICE netlists, KiCad S-expressions).

stories.title: DPO eval: SFT vs SFT+DPO comparison
stories.description: Benchmark SFT-only vs SFT+DPO adapters on all 10 niches. 480B as judge. Output: results/dpo-eval.json with per-domain accuracy delta. If DPO doesn't improve, document why and keep SFT-only.

stories.title: GRPO reward functions for niche domains
stories.description: Create src/eval/reward_functions.py with domain-specific reward functions: syntax_valid (SPICE/KiCad parseable), format_correct (expected structure), accuracy_reward (480B judge score). Each function: prompt + response → float score 0-1. Tests: tests/test_reward_functions.py.

stories.title: GRPO training script (mlx-tune GRPOTrainer)
stories.description: Create scripts/train_grpo_niches.py using mlx-tune GRPOTrainer or MLX-GRPO. For domains needing chain-of-thought (spice, emc, embedded, power). Reward: syntax_valid + accuracy_reward. Group size 4, KL penalty 0.1. Output: outputs/stacks/stack-<domain>-grpo/.

stories.title: GRPO training 4 reasoning domains
stories.description: Run GRPO on spice, emc, embedded, power (domains requiring multi-step reasoning). Compare: SFT → SFT+DPO → SFT+DPO+GRPO chain. Expected: better chain-of-thought on design problems.

stories.title: Domain-specific embedding model
stories.description: Fine-tune embedding model (MiniLM or Qwen3-Embedding via mlx-tune EmbeddingSFTTrainer) on niche domain text. Training data: all merged datasets as positive pairs (same-domain = similar, cross-domain = dissimilar). Output: models/niche-embeddings/. Replace hash-based stub in src/memory/aeon.py.

stories.title: Wire embeddings into Aeon memory
stories.description: Replace AeonPalace._default_embed (hash-based) with the fine-tuned embedding model. Update Atlas to use real embeddings. Benchmark: recall@10 on 1000 episodes before/after. Expected: +20-30% recall accuracy on niche domain queries.

stories.title: Eval: full pipeline with fine-tuned embeddings
stories.description: End-to-end eval: query → Aeon recall (with fine-tuned embeddings) → domain routing → inference → response quality. Compare vs hash-based embeddings. Output: results/embedding-eval.json.
